channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-113] Dependency issues with RHEL installation - lustre-ldiskfs
item.description:
Installing 2.1 at LLNL for testing, encountered the following collision:

---> Package lustre-ldiskfs.x86_64 0:3.3.0-2.6.18_194.17.1.el5_lustre.g94d9119_gd5e659b set to be updated
--> Processing Dependency: ldiskfsprogs >= 1.41.12.2.ora1 for package: lustre-ldiskfs
--> Running transaction check
--> Processing Dependency: e2fsprogs = 1.39-23.el5 for package: infinipath-psm
---> Package e2fsprogs.x86_64 0:1.41.14.wc1-0redhat set to be updated
--> Processing Dependency: libcom_err.so.2 for package: krb5-libs

libcom_err.so.2 is in our e2fsprogs package.
On RedHat, libcom_err.so.2 is in a separate e2fsprogs-lib package.

This may be the result of the packaging mis-match between our e2fsprogs package and
the RHEL setup.
item.summary: Dependency issues with RHEL installation - lustre-ldiskfs

status.description: The issue is considered finished, the resolution is correct. Issues which are closed can be reopened.

comments.comment:
Integrated in lustre-master » i686,server,el6,inkernel #207

{idl,user}

.h nice to userspace

Toro nodes pull the latest successful e2fsprogs-master builds now. Documented in TT-69.

LU-113: add .spec file for RHEL6 split packages

Andreas Dilger : 8593f662e9e613785c0d46ede1834215c8060f6d

Integrated in e2fsprogs-master » x86_64,el5 #19
LU-113: add .spec file for RHEL6 split packages

Andreas Dilger : 8593f662e9e613785c0d46ede1834215c8060f6d
Files :

  • patches/ChangeLog
  • patches/e2fsprogs-attr_cleanup.patch
  • patches/e2fsprogs-lfsck.patch
  • patches/e2fsprogs-specdotin.patch
  • patches/e2fsprogs-1.40.4-sb_feature_check_ignore.patch

  • patches/e2fsprogs-e2scan.patch

  • patches/e2fsprogs-large-ea.patch
  • patches/e2fsprogs-fiemap-lustre.patch
  • patches/e2fsprogs-rpm_support.patch
  • patches/sles/libcom_err-no-static-buffer.patch
  • patches/e2fsprogs-data_in_dirent.patch
  • patches/e2fsprogs-mmp.patch
I'll work on making the lab nodes pull the fixed RPMs tonight or tomorrow at the latest.
RPMs were build for x86_64/el5 and x86_64/el6. An existing problem is preventing the i686 RPMs from being built.
Patch has been merged, closing bug. I don't know how to get the resulting RPMs into the test node images, however...

Build #12 (Apr 29, 2011 10:34:36 AM) of e2fsprogs provides ldiskfs.

This resolves a dependency requirement that lustre-ldiskfs had.

Integrated in e2fsprogs-reviews » x86_64,el5 #12
LU-113: add .spec file for RHEL6 split packages

Andreas Dilger :
Files :

  • patches/ChangeLog
  • patches/e2fsprogs-misc.patch
  • patches/e2fsprogs-data_in_dirent.patch
  • patches/e2fsprogs-rpm_RHEL-6.patch
  • patches/series
  • patches/e2fsprogs-1.40.4-sb_feature_check_ignore.patch
  • patches/e2fsprogs-mmp.patch
  • patches/e2fsprogs-lfsck.patch
  • patches/e2fsprogs-attr_cleanup.patch
  • patches/e2fsprogs-rpm_support.patch
  • patches/sles/libcom_err-no-static-buffer.patch
  • patches/e2fsprogs-e2scan.patch
  • patches/e2fsprogs-large-ea.patch
  • patches/e2fsprogs-fiemap-lustre.patch
  • patches/e2fsprogs-specdotin.patch
  • patches/e2fsprogs-rpm_SLES-11.patch

It looks like the most recent build (#11) for x86_64/el6 completed successfully, while the first one for this patch (#8) for x86_64/el5 also passed, but has failed for all subsequent builds due to some kind of problem in the test harness, AFAICS:

http://newbuild.whamcloud.com/job/e2fsprogs-reviews/11/arch=x86_64,distro=el5/console
:
:
+ rm 'lustre-source-[0-9]*'
rm: cannot remove `lustre-source-[0-9]*': No such file or directory
+ true
+ curl -L -O http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-master/build_type=server,distro=el5,arch=x86_64,ib_stack=inkernel/lastSuccessfulBuild/artifact/artifacts/RPMS/x86_64/
curl: Remote file name has no length!
curl: try 'curl --help' for more information
+ echo 'error trying to get latest lustre source rpm'
error trying to get latest lustre source rpm

The i686 builds are all failing due to internal regression test failures in f_messy_inode, which is already described in LU-193 and predates any changes I made.

In any case, I believe that change 472 is ready to land so that RHEL6 testing can continue.

Robert doesn't really have much to do with Jenkins these days. I've re-triggered change 472 with the combination filter removed. The expectation now is that e2fsprogs-reviews should build successfully on 32/64-bit EL5/EL6.
Robert, this only built for RHEL5, but Jenkins needs to be updated to also add do the RHEL6 build to test this patch.

Integrated in e2fsprogs-reviews » x86_64,el5 #7
LU-113: add .spec file for RHEL6 split packages

Andreas Dilger :
Files :

  • patches/e2fsprogs-misc.patch
  • patches/e2fsprogs-data_in_dirent.patch
  • patches/sles/libcom_err-no-static-buffer.patch
  • patches/e2fsprogs-large-ea.patch
  • patches/e2fsprogs-e2scan.patch
  • patches/e2fsprogs-rpm_support.patch
  • patches/e2fsprogs-lfsck.patch
  • patches/e2fsprogs-mmp.patch
  • patches/e2fsprogs-rpm_RHEL-6.patch
  • patches/e2fsprogs-rpm_SLES-11.patch
  • patches/e2fsprogs-1.40.4-sb_feature_check_ignore.patch
  • patches/series
  • patches/e2fsprogs-specdotin.patch
  • patches/ChangeLog

There are two of solutions to the problem of conflicting packages today:

  • update our e2fsprogs.spec.in file to conditionally split the e2fsprogs RPM
    for RHEL6, so that we package a separate e2fsprogs-libs RPM, and disable
    libcom_err, libuuid, etc.
  • use the upstream RHEL6 e2fsprogs.spec.in file and patch it to add in ldiskfs,
    e2scan, etc as needed, as Brian suggested.

I haven't actually looked at the two options to see which one might be easier
to implement.

Andreas

Could you please comment?

Thanks

Peter
since this issue prevents 2.1 release testing on RHEL6, set it to blocker.

Our latest version of lustre e2fsprogs (version 1.41.90.wc1) contains an upstream fix for a problem seen in Fedora:
Description of problem:

Some pieces of software that use libcom_err fail to find it
during the configure step because the library is in /lib64
while the header file is in /usr/include/et instead of
/usr/include. While there is only one Fedora package at
present that requires libcom_err-devel (krb5-devel), this
sometimes causes difficulty in building unpackaged software
and requires manually setting CFLAGS.

This version should be tested to see if it fixes some of the upgrade problems.

Bruce,

I discussed and tried the '-U' option with e2fsprogs on Friday with Cliff when I was doing this testing:

"...with the U it succeeded - and subsequently ldiskfs installed without error ..."

Apologies: I forgot to add that note to is thread when I did this.

From the Oracle's Lustre Operation Manual:

If e2fsprogs is already installed on your Linux system, install the Lustre-specific e2fsprogs version by using rpm -Uvh to update the existing e2fsprogs package.

For example:
$ rpm Uvh e2fsprogs<ver>

The rpm command options --force or --nodeps are not required to install or update the Lustre-specific e2fsprogs package. We specifically recommend that you not use these options. If errors are reported, notify Lustre Support by filing a bug.

Base machine is CentOS5, with wc lustre kernel, modules, ldiskfs, e2fs rpms installed from: http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-master-centos5/

[root@client-11 ~]# rpm -iv ./lustre-tests-2.0.59-2.6.18_194.17.1.el5_lustre.g94d9119_g1cbf25c.x86_64.rpm 
error: Failed dependencies:
	lustre = 2.0.59 is needed by lustre-tests-2.0.59-2.6.18_194.17.1.el5_lustre.g94d9119_g1cbf25c.x86_64

Same lustre kernel onto of CentOS5 vanilla install:

[root@client-10 ~]# rpm -iv ./e2fsprogs-1.41.14.wc1-0redhat.x86_64.rpm 
Preparing packages for installation...
	file /lib64/libblkid.so.1.0 from install of e2fsprogs-1.41.14.wc1-0redhat.x86_64 conflicts with file from package e2fsprogs-libs-1.39-23.el5_5.1.x86_64
	file /lib64/libcom_err.so.2.1 from install of e2fsprogs-1.41.14.wc1-0redhat.x86_64 conflicts with file from package e2fsprogs-libs-1.39-23.el5_5.1.x86_64
	file /lib64/libe2p.so.2.3 from install of e2fsprogs-1.41.14.wc1-0redhat.x86_64 conflicts with file from package e2fsprogs-libs-1.39-23.el5_5.1.x86_64
	file /lib64/libext2fs.so.2.4 from install of e2fsprogs-1.41.14.wc1-0redhat.x86_64 conflicts with file from package e2fsprogs-libs-1.39-23.el5_5.1.x86_64
	file /lib64/libss.so.2.0 from install of e2fsprogs-1.41.14.wc1-0redhat.x86_64 conflicts with file from package e2fsprogs-libs-1.39-23.el5_5.1.x86_64
	file /lib64/libuuid.so.1.2 from install of e2fsprogs-1.41.14.wc1-0redhat.x86_64 conflicts with file from package e2fsprogs-libs-1.39-23.el5_5.1.x86_64
	file /etc/mke2fs.conf from install of e2fsprogs-1.41.14.wc1-0redhat.x86_64 conflicts with file from package e2fsprogs-1.39-23.el5_5.1.x86_64
	file /sbin/badblocks from install of e2fsprogs-1.41.14.wc1-0redhat.x86_64 conflicts with file from package e2fsprogs-1.39-23.el5_5.1.x86_64
	file /sbin/blkid from install of e2fsprogs-1.41.14.wc1-0redhat.x86_64 conflicts with file from package e2fsprogs-1.39-23.el5_5.1.x86_64
	file /sbin/debugfs from install of e2fsprogs-1.41.14.wc1-0redhat.x86_64 conflicts with file from package e2fsprogs-1.39-23.el5_5.1.x86_64
	file /sbin/dumpe2fs from install of e2fsprogs-1.41.14.wc1-0redhat.x86_64 conflicts with file from package e2fsprogs-1.39-23.el5_5.1.x86_64
	file /sbin/e2fsck from install of e2fsprogs-1.41.14.wc1-0redhat.x86_64 conflicts with file from package e2fsprogs-1.39-23.el5_5.1.x86_64
	file /sbin/e2image from install of e2fsprogs-1.41.14.wc1-0redhat.x86_64 conflicts with file from package e2fsprogs-1.39-23.el5_5.1.x86_64
	file /sbin/e2label from install of e2fsprogs-1.41.14.wc1-0redhat.x86_64 conflicts with file from package e2fsprogs-1.39-23.el5_5.1.x86_64
	file /sbin/findfs from install of e2fsprogs-1.41.14.wc1-0redhat.x86_64 conflicts with file from package e2fsprogs-1.39-23.el5_5.1.x86_64
	file /sbin/fsck from install of e2fsprogs-1.41.14.wc1-0redhat.x86_64 conflicts with file from package e2fsprogs-1.39-23.el5_5.1.x86_64
	file /sbin/fsck.ext2 from install of e2fsprogs-1.41.14.wc1-0redhat.x86_64 conflicts with file from package e2fsprogs-1.39-23.el5_5.1.x86_64
	file /sbin/fsck.ext3 from install of e2fsprogs-1.41.14.wc1-0redhat.x86_64 conflicts with file from package e2fsprogs-1.39-23.el5_5.1.x86_64
	file /sbin/logsave from install of e2fsprogs-1.41.14.wc1-0redhat.x86_64 conflicts with file from package e2fsprogs-1.39-23.el5_5.1.x86_64
	file /sbin/mke2fs from install of e2fsprogs-1.41.14.wc1-0redhat.x86_64 conflicts with file from package e2fsprogs-1.39-23.el5_5.1.x86_64
	file /sbin/mkfs.ext2 from install of e2fsprogs-1.41.14.wc1-0redhat.x86_64 conflicts with file from package e2fsprogs-1.39-23.el5_5.1.x86_64
	file /sbin/mkfs.ext3 from install of e2fsprogs-1.41.14.wc1-0redhat.x86_64 conflicts with file from package e2fsprogs-1.39-23.el5_5.1.x86_64
	file /sbin/resize2fs from install of e2fsprogs-1.41.14.wc1-0redhat.x86_64 conflicts with file from package e2fsprogs-1.39-23.el5_5.1.x86_64

	file /usr/share/man/man8/mkfs.ext2.8.gz from install of e2fsprogs-1.41.14.wc1-0redhat.x86_64 conflicts with file from package e2fsprogs-1.39-23.el5_5.1.x86_64
	file /usr/share/man/man8/mkfs.ext3.8.gz from install of e2fsprogs-1.41.14.wc1-0redhat.x86_64 conflicts with file from package e2fsprogs-1.39-23.el5_5.1.x86_64
	file /usr/share/man/man8/mklost+found.8.gz from install of e2fsprogs-1.41.14.wc1-0redhat.x86_64 conflicts with file from package e2fsprogs-1.39-23.el5_5.1.x86_64
	file /usr/share/man/man8/resize2fs.8.gz from install of e2fsprogs-1.41.14.wc1-0redhat.x86_64 conflicts with file from package e2fsprogs-1.39-23.el5_5.1.x86_64
	file /usr/share/man/man8/tune2fs.8.gz from install of e2fsprogs-1.41.14.wc1-0redhat.x86_64 conflicts with file from package e2fsprogs-1.39-23.el5_5.1.x86_64

Taking a CentOS5, provisioned on Toro, after installing the kernel from:
http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-master-centos5/lastSuccessfulBuild/artifact/BUILD/RPMS/x86_64/kernel-2.6.18-194.17.1.el5_lustre.g94d9119.x86_64.rpm

[root@client-10 ~]# rpm -iv ./lustre-ldiskfs-3.3.0-2.6.18_194.17.1.el5_lustre.g94d9119_g1cbf25c.x86_64.rpm 
error: Failed dependencies:
	ldiskfsprogs >= 1.41.12.2.ora1 is needed by lustre-ldiskfs-3.3.0-2.6.18_194.17.1.el5_lustre.g94d9119_g1cbf25c.x86_64
[root@client-10 ~]#

It seems like we need to start building e2fsprogs on rhel/centos using the vendor's spec rather than our feeble attempt at a single spec that keeps all distros happy. There is framework in our e2fsprogs patches for this already and IIRC, the sles11 build utilizes this, for an example to work from.

Currently to build images at LLNL I must use rpm --nodeps --force
to install e2fsprogs and lustre-ldiskfs. So the image creation involves an
additional hand-hacking step atm.

Currently to build images at LLNL I must use rpm --nodeps --force
to install e2fsprogs and lustre-ldiskfs. So the image creation involves an
additional hand-hacking step atm.

You could try using --skip-broken to work around the problem

Is that the entire output of the yum transaction? It seems to be missing any specific errors. Can you paste the entire output into this bug?

There looks to be a couple of problems:

--> Processing Dependency: e2fsprogs = 1.39-23.el5 for package: infinipath-psm

That's a dependency we are never going to be able to satisfy if we continue to lead ahead of RHEL's own released version of e2fsprogs. It's likely that it's a case of "too strict" a dependency in infinipath-psm and needs to be relaxed.

As for the libcom_err.so.2 and krb5-libs message, I'm not really clear what it's telling us, which is why I suspect that I'm not seeing the entire output from the yum transaction.

There is framework in the e2fsprogs repo for making a release that matches the vendor's release by using the vendor's e2fsprogs SPEC file. I believe that this is how the SLES releases are done. Making this happen for Redhat is something that the e2fsprogs maintainer needs to do and maintain.

Sorry, incomplete.

  • the libcom_err.so.2 change collides with a bunch
    of packages, starting with krb5-libs, causing failure of the
    installation.
